parentQuestion: What type of cells line the ventricles of the brain?

scaffolds.explain: The VENTRICLES are FLUID-FILLED CAVITIES inside the brain. There are four ventricles (two lateral, third, and fourth) connected and filled with cerebrospinal fluid (CSF).

wrongExplains.0: The ventricles are cavities, not solid tissue.
wrongExplains.2: Blood vessels are separate structures. Ventricles are CSF-filled cavities.
wrongExplains.3: Ventricles are inside the brain, not part of the skull.

options: The only cells in the brain
Support cells that help neurons but don't transmit electrical signals
scaffolds.explain: GLIAL CELLS are SUPPORT CELLS in the nervous system. They provide nutrition, protection, and maintenance but don't transmit electrical signals like neurons do.

wrongExplains.0: The brain also has neurons. Glial cells are the support cells.
wrongExplains.2: Glial cells are nervous system cells, not blood cells.
wrongExplains.3: Glial cells are neural tissue, not muscle.

options: Glial cells that line the ventricles
scaffolds.explain: EPENDYMAL CELLS are a type of GLIAL CELL that forms the lining of the ventricles. They're epithelial-like support cells specialized for the ventricular surface.

wrongExplains.0: Neurons transmit signals. Ependymal cells are glial cells that line ventricles.
wrongExplains.2: Ependymal cells are neural tissue, not bone.
wrongExplains.3: Ependymal cells line ventricles. Blood cells circulate in vessels.

scaffolds.q: What special feature do ependymal cells have?
options: They have cilia that help circulate CSF
scaffolds.explain: Ependymal cells have CILIA on their surface that beat in coordinated waves. This helps CIRCULATE CSF through the ventricular system.

wrongExplains.0: Neurons generally don't regenerate. Ependymal cells help with CSF circulation.
wrongExplains.2: Blood cells are produced in bone marrow. Ependymal cells produce/circulate CSF.
wrongExplains.3: Ependymal cells are soft neural tissue, not bone.

options: Specialized ependymal cells that produce cerebrospinal fluid
scaffolds.explain: The CHOROID PLEXUS contains modified ependymal cells that filter blood plasma and actively PRODUCE CSF — about 500mL per day. It's located in the ventricles.

wrongExplains.0: The choroid plexus is a CSF-producing structure, not a brain lobe.
wrongExplains.2: The choroid plexus is soft tissue in the ventricles, not bone.
wrongExplains.3: The choroid plexus contains capillaries but is mainly specialized ependymal tissue.

options: No, neurons transmit signals — lining cavities is a job for epithelial-type cells
scaffolds.explain: NEURONS DO NOT LINE CAVITIES — they transmit electrical signals. Lining cavities is the job of epithelial-type cells. In the brain, ependymal cells (glial) line the ventricles.

wrongExplains.0: Neurons never line cavities. That's not their function.
wrongExplains.2: The heart is lined by endocardium, not neurons.
wrongExplains.3: The lungs are lined by respiratory epithelium, not neurons.

options: Yes, astrocytes line all brain cavities
No, astrocytes support neurons in brain tissue but don't form the ventricular lining
scaffolds.explain: ASTROCYTES support neurons throughout brain tissue and contribute to the blood-brain barrier, but they DON'T FORM THE VENTRICULAR LINING. Ependymal cells do that.

wrongExplains.0: Ependymal cells, not astrocytes, line the ventricles.
wrongExplains.2: Ependymal cells line ventricles. Astrocytes are in brain parenchyma.
wrongExplains.3: Astrocytes definitely exist — they're important glial cells.

scaffolds.q: What cells produce and circulate CSF?
scaffolds.explain: EPENDYMAL CELLS produce CSF (in the choroid plexus) and circulate it (with their cilia). They're the CSF specialists lining the ventricular system.

wrongExplains.0: Neurons transmit signals. Ependymal cells handle CSF.
wrongExplains.2: Red blood cells carry oxygen. Ependymal cells produce CSF.
wrongExplains.3: Bone cells make bone. Ependymal cells are nervous system cells.

scaffolds.q: What type of cells line the ventricles of the brain?
scaffolds.explain: EPENDYMAL CELLS line the ventricles. They're ciliated glial cells that help produce and circulate CSF. Not neurons (they transmit signals), not astrocytes (they support neurons in tissue).

wrongExplains.0: Neurons transmit electrical signals. They don't line cavities.
wrongExplains.2: Astrocytes support neurons in brain tissue. They don't line the ventricles.
wrongExplains.3: Red blood cells are in blood vessels, not lining ventricles.
